title: Counting loops should bound the counter with a relational comparison
messageFormat: Use a relational comparison (< or >) rather than equality to bound this loop counter.
summary: Driving a counting for loop with an equality test such as i != limit assumes the counter lands exactly on the boundary. If the step ever skips past it, or the limit is recomputed, the loop runs far longer than intended.
A relational comparison stops the loop as soon as the counter reaches the bound, which is the safe and conventional way to express the range.
